paragraphs.text: Joseph tended the sheep with the sons of the concubines and brought to his father an ill report of them. And for this, that he revealed their faults, the brothers hated him. And Joseph saw two dreams: the first of the sheaves, and the second of the sun, moon, and twelve stars bowing down to him. For these dreams the brothers hated him still more. And they mocked his dreams and said: “Will Rachel too, who is dead, come and bow down before him?” But they did not know that man and wife are one body. And when Jacob, represented under the image of the sun, bowed down to the top of Joseph’s staff, then in him also Rachel bowed down, represented under the image of the moon, though she did not really bow down.

Jacob sent Joseph to the flocks, to bring back news of his brothers. But the brothers, instead of news of Joseph, sent back to their father his coat stained with blood, and Joseph without mercy they cast into a pit in the wilderness. At home they wept tearfully for him. They sold him naked to the Arabians, but among the Canaanites they cried over him. They put bonds on Joseph’s hands and feet and sent him on his way, while they themselves, sitting quietly at home, performed mourning for him. So Joseph came into Egypt, was sold there, and in a short time changed two masters.
